skill.name: paid-ads
skill.description: Provides structured guidance for planning, writing, targeting, testing, and optimizing paid advertising campaigns across major ad platforms.
skill.summary: Plan and improve paid media campaigns with practical frameworks for targeting, creative, budgets, and reporting.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external commands, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance indicators, but the reviewed lines are Markdown examples and paid advertising terminology. No executable code, network requests, credential access, or prompt-injection text was found. One low-risk semantic issue remains because SKILL.md claims direct access to ad platform accounts.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detections
low_findings.description: The static Ruby and shell backtick detections occur inside Markdown code fences that show campaign structure and ad copy templates. They are not executable commands and do not invoke a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are visible Markdown examples and placeholders. No command interpreter, script, or execution instruction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts map to ordinary advertising language such as Meta, setup guides, future-state copy, job titles, bid strategies, and common mistakes. No cryptographic function, system probing instruction, or host enumeration appears in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited terms are embedded in paid advertising guidance, not code. The file contains no crypto API usage or system reconnaissance workflow.

low_findings.title: Misleading Platform Account Access Claim
low_findings.description: The skill states that the assistant has direct access to ad platform accounts. This can cause the assistant to overstate its capabilities or imply authorization to change accounts, although the file itself does not implement platform access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The capability claim is explicit in the source text. The risk is semantic rather than executable because no connectors, credentials, or API calls are present.

content.user_title: Plan and Optimize Paid Ad Campaigns
content.value_statement: Paid advertising requires clear targeting, creative testing, and budget control. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured campaign guidance.
actual_capabilities: Selects suitable ad platforms based on audience, offer, and campaign goal.
Builds campaign structures with naming conventions, budget splits, and testing phases.
Drafts ad copy using PAS, BAB, social proof, headline, and CTA frameworks.
Recommends audience targeting for Google Ads, Meta Ads, LinkedIn Ads, and retargeting.
Provides optimization checklists for CPA, ROAS, CTR, CPM, creative fatigue, and reporting.
limitations: Does not log in to ad platforms or change live campaigns by itself.
Does not verify current platform policies, prices, or interface changes automatically.
Requires user-provided campaign data for accurate performance diagnosis.
Does not replace legal, privacy, or regulated-industry advertising review.

use_cases.title: Launch a First PPC Campaign
use_cases.description: Create a practical starting structure for goals, budgets, targeting, creative, and measurement.

use_cases.title: Improve Underperforming Ads
use_cases.description: Diagnose high CPA, low CTR, weak conversion rate, or rising CPM with focused next steps.

use_cases.title: Plan B2B Paid Media
use_cases.description: Map LinkedIn, Google, and retargeting tactics to a high-value business audience.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Paid Ads Platform
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose the best paid advertising platform for this offer: [offer]. My audience is [audience], budget is [budget], and goal is [goal].

prompt_templates.title: Create Campaign Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a paid ads campaign structure for [platform]. Include campaigns, ad sets or groups, naming conventions, budget allocation, and initial tests.

prompt_templates.title: Write and Test Ad Copy
prompt_templates.prompt: Write ad copy for [product] using PAS, BAB, and social proof angles. Include headlines, primary text, CTAs, and testing recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Performance Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these paid ads metrics: [metrics]. Identify likely issues, prioritize fixes, and recommend the next experiments for CPA and ROAS improvement.

output_examples.input: Plan a Meta Ads campaign for a project management SaaS with a free trial.
output_examples.output: A campaign plan with conversion objective, prospecting and retargeting ad sets, three creative angles, budget split, and a weekly review checklist.

output_examples.input: Our Google Ads CPA is too high and CTR is low.
output_examples.output: A diagnostic list covering search intent, keyword match types, negative keywords, ad relevance, landing page fit, and bid strategy changes.

output_examples.input: Create LinkedIn ad ideas for a B2B analytics whitepaper.
output_examples.output: Audience segments, sponsored content angles, lead gen form notes, headline options, and follow-up testing priorities.

best_practices: Provide clear goals, budget, audience, landing page, and current performance metrics before asking for optimization.
Use the skill to plan tests with one main variable at a time.
Review recommendations against current platform policies before publishing ads.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to access, modify, or spend from an ad account without authorized tooling.
Do not treat generated ad copy as final compliance-approved language.
Do not optimize only for clicks when the business goal is leads, sales, or retained revenue.

faq.question: Can this skill create live ads in my account?
faq.answer: No. It provides planning, copy, targeting, and optimization guidance. Live account changes require separate authorized platform access.

faq.question: Which ad platforms does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers Google Ads, Meta Ads, LinkedIn Ads, X Ads, TikTok Ads, and common retargeting workflows.

faq.question: Can it help with ad copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes frameworks for primary text, headlines, CTAs, social proof, and creative testing.

faq.question: Can it analyze campaign performance?
faq.answer: Yes, if you provide metrics such as spend, CPA, ROAS, CTR, CPC, CPM, conversions, and landing page conversion rate.

faq.question: Does it replace a media buyer?
faq.answer: No. It supports planning and analysis, but experienced review is important for budget, compliance, and business context.

faq.question: Is this useful for small budgets?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes testing guidance, budget allocation, and platform selection that can fit early-stage campaigns.
